name: hybrid-classify-alerts
description: Alert-triage brain Phase 2: the severity Smart-Classifier pass. Sits after an alert normalizer running with EMIT_OPS_ALERTS=false (which hands off a plain {"alerts":[...]} array) and becomes the sole __ops_alert__ emitter. For each alert it builds the canonical feature text (Title/Source/Resource), consults the distilled ops-severity model first (talos:core/model predict-batch, lifecycle-gated server-side), and routes ONLY the alerts the model isn't confident about to the LLM (few-shot anchored on human corrections, <untrusted_data> spotlighting). It stamps each alert's severity_hint with the predicted severity, keeps severity_raw untouched, and re-wraps the whole array under __ops_alert__ so the engine ingests it. LLM-labeled alerts emit an __ml_distill__ envelope (active learning; example_key = the alert's dedup_key). Degradation is soft: an alert failing both legs keeps its original severity_hint, and non-classifiable entries (state=closed recovery status_events, which have no title) pass through untouched. severity_hint still applies on FIRST ingest only — human corrections outrank it.

MODEL_NAME.description: Registered ml_models name of the severity classifier (e.g. 'ops-severity'). Resolved under the execution user's tenancy; served only once the model reaches hybrid/fast_primary (shadow/llm_only → abstain-all → the LLM leg handles everything). Its labels are the fixed six ops severities.

SYSTEM_PROMPT.description: The severity rubric for the LLM fallback leg — how to weigh a critical/high/medium/low/info/noise call. Must instruct the model to return {"label":"<one severity>"}. Alert content is wrapped in <untrusted_data> (spotlighting) with an anti-injection directive before the LLM sees it.

MODEL.description: LLM model identifier for the fallback leg.

PROVIDER.description: LLM provider for the fallback leg. 'ollama' (default, Tier 1, local) keeps alert content on-host.

MAX_TOKENS.description: Max tokens for each single-label LLM severity response.
